title: SpaceX Successfully Launches Massive Starship V3 Rocket
subtitle: The largest rocket in history completes a major test flight, bringing humanity closer to future moon missions.

summary.en: SpaceX has successfully launched its new Starship V3 rocket from Texas. The massive vehicle is the largest and most powerful rocket ever built. Although the flight experienced some engine problems, it reached its main goals and landed in the Indian Ocean. This success is important for future NASA missions to the moon. The launch also comes at a key time for SpaceX as the company prepares for a major stock market debut.
summary.zh: 太空探索科技公司已成功從德州發射其新型星艦第三代火箭。這枚巨大的載具是史上建造過最大且最強大的火箭。儘管飛行過程中經歷了一些引擎問題，但仍達成了主要目標並降落在印度洋。這項成功對於美國國家航空暨太空總署未來的登月任務至關重要。此次發射也正值太空探索科技公司準備進行重大股票上市的關鍵時刻。

paragraphs.en: SpaceX has reached a new milestone in space travel. On Friday, the company launched its Starship V3 rocket from its base in South Texas. Standing at 124 meters tall, or more than 40 storeys high, it is the largest and most powerful rocket in history. According to BBC News, the launch happened just after 17:30 local time following a short delay. The first attempt on Thursday was stopped because of a problem with the launch tower.
paragraphs.zh: 太空探索科技公司（SpaceX）在太空旅行領域達成了一項新的里程碑。週五，該公司從位於南德州的基地發射了星艦第三代（Starship V3）火箭。這枚火箭高達一百二十四公尺，相當於四十多層樓高，是史上最大且最強大的火箭。根據英國廣播公司新聞網（BBC News）報導，在經歷短暫延遲後，發射於當地時間十七點三十分後進行。週四的首次嘗試因發射塔出現問題而被迫中止。

paragraphs.en: This mission was the 12th flight of a SpaceX rocket. Ars Technica reports that the rocket is powered by 33 methane-fueled engines. After lifting off, the rocket traveled over the Gulf of Mexico. Once it reached space, it released 20 dummy satellites. About an hour after the launch, the rocket landed in the Indian Ocean. As planned, the vehicle exploded upon hitting the water. While the mission was largely successful, both stages of the rocket did experience some engine failures during the flight.
paragraphs.zh: 本次任務是太空探索科技公司火箭的第十二次飛行。科技媒體 Ars Technica 報導指出，該火箭由三十三具以甲烷為燃料的引擎驅動。火箭升空後飛越墨西哥灣，抵達太空後釋放了二十枚模擬衛星。發射約一小時後，火箭降落在印度洋。正如計畫所預期，載具在觸水時發生爆炸。儘管任務整體相當成功，但火箭的兩節推進器在飛行過程中確實經歷了部分引擎故障。

paragraphs.en: Despite these small issues, the results were much better than previous versions. Ars Technica noted that the first versions of Starship, known as V1 and V2, both broke apart during their first flights in 2023 and 2025. Because Starship V3 survived its first flight, the company and its partners feel more confident. This success is very important for NASA, which plans to use the Starship vehicle for future missions to the moon. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman watched the launch in person and praised the team, stating on social media that the mission brought the world one step closer to the moon and Mars.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管存在這些小問題，結果仍遠優於先前版本。Ars Technica 指出，被稱為第一代與第二代的星艦初期版本，皆在二零二三年與二零二五年的首飛中解體。由於星艦第三代成功完成了首次飛行，該公司及其合作夥伴感到更有信心。這項成功對美國國家航空暨太空總署（NASA）至關重要，該署計畫將星艦載具用於未來的登月任務。美國國家航空暨太空總署署長賈瑞德·艾薩克曼（Jared Isaacman）親自觀看了發射並讚揚團隊，他在社群媒體上表示，這項任務讓世界距離月球與火星又更近了一步。

paragraphs.en: Elon Musk, the founder and CEO of SpaceX, celebrated the achievement on the social media platform X. He congratulated his team, calling the launch an "epic" success and saying they had scored a "goal for humanity." Gwynne Shotwell, the second in command at SpaceX, also thanked the team for their hard work. She noted that the future of flying among the stars feels much closer after this successful debut.
paragraphs.zh: 太空探索科技公司創辦人兼執行長伊隆·馬斯克（Elon Musk）在社群媒體平台 X 上慶祝了這項成就。他祝賀團隊，稱此次發射為「史詩級」的成功，並表示他們為「全人類踢進了一球」。太空探索科技公司的二把手關恩·修特威爾（Gwynne Shotwell）也感謝團隊的辛勤付出。她指出，在這次成功的首秀後，星際飛行的未來感覺更加觸手可及。

paragraphs.en: This test flight comes at a very busy time for the company. BBC News reports that SpaceX is preparing for an initial public offering (IPO) — a process where a private company first sells shares to the public on the stock market. This is expected to be the largest stock market debut in the history of Wall Street. Because of the large number of shares Elon Musk owns, the company’s high value of $1.25 trillion could make him the first-ever trillionaire. Beyond building rockets, the company also operates the Starlink satellite internet service and owns an artificial intelligence firm called xAI.
paragraphs.zh: 此次試飛正值該公司極為忙碌的時期。英國廣播公司新聞網報導，太空探索科技公司正準備進行首次公開募股（IPO），即一家私人企業首次在股票市場向大眾出售股份的過程。這預計將成為華爾街歷史上規模最大的上市案。由於伊隆·馬斯克持有大量股份，該公司高達一點二五兆美元的估值，可能使他成為史上首位兆萬富翁。除了製造火箭，該公司還經營星鏈（Starlink）衛星網路服務，並擁有一家名為 xAI 的人工智慧公司。

paragraphs.en: The road to this launch was not easy. Ars Technica reported that there was a gap of more than seven months between this flight and the last test in October. This was the longest wait between flights since the program began in 2023. During this time, engineers worked to finish a second launch pad and performed many tests on the ground. These ground tests also faced their own challenges, which delayed the schedule.
paragraphs.zh: 通往此次發射的道路並不容易。Ars Technica 報導稱，本次飛行與去年十月的上次測試之間，間隔了超過七個月。這是自二零二三年計畫開始以來，兩次飛行之間最長的等待時間。在此期間，工程師致力於完成第二座發射台，並在地面進行了多次測試。這些地面測試也面臨各自的挑戰，進而延誤了時程。

paragraphs.en: Looking ahead, the success of Starship V3 provides a strong foundation for future space exploration. By proving that the new design can survive the journey to space and return, SpaceX has shown that its technology is moving in the right direction. As the company prepares for its massive stock market entry, the focus will likely remain on refining the rocket’s performance and preparing for more complex missions. For now, the successful splashdown in the Indian Ocean marks a significant victory for the engineers and for the future of human space travel.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，星艦第三代的成功為未來的太空探索奠定了堅實基礎。透過證明新設計能夠在太空旅程中存活並返回，太空探索科技公司展現了其技術正朝正確方向發展。隨著該公司為大規模上市做準備，焦點可能會持續放在精進火箭性能與準備更複雜的任務上。目前，成功在印度洋濺落，對工程師以及人類太空旅行的未來而言，皆標誌著一場重大的勝利。

keyTerms.definitionEn: The person who is next in rank to the leader of an organization.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是一個常見的職位描述，容易被誤認為是軍事用語，但在企業中也常用。文中：Gwynne Shotwell, the second in command at SpaceX, also thanked the team for their hard work.

keyTerms.definitionEn: The first public appearance or performance of something.
keyTerms.noteZh: 此詞常與表演連結，但在商業與科技新聞中常指產品或公司的首次公開。文中：She noted that the future of flying among the stars feels much closer after this successful debut.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To perform tests on the surface of the earth rather than in flight.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（地面），這裡作動詞形容測試的性質。文中：These ground tests also faced their own challenges, which delayed the schedule.

sources.name: BBC News
sources.title: SpaceX launches massive Starship V3 rocket on test flight

sources.name: Ars Technica
sources.title: SpaceX's Starship V3—still a work in progress—mostly successful on first flight

quiz.question: What caused the delay in the initial launch attempt of the Starship V3 rocket?

options.A: Severe weather conditions in South Texas.
options.B: A technical issue involving the launch tower.
options.C: The need to perform more ground tests on the engines.
options.D: A failure to release the dummy satellites correctly.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第一段最後一句：「The first attempt on Thursday was stopped because of a problem with the launch tower.」明確指出是因為發射塔的問題導致延遲。選項 A 文中未提及天氣因素；選項 C 是後來在第六段提到的測試背景，而非第一次延遲的原因；選項 D 是飛行過程中的任務，與發射延遲無關。

quiz.question: Based on the article, why is the success of the Starship V3 flight particularly significant for NASA?

options.A: It proves that SpaceX can operate as a publicly traded company.
options.B: It confirms that the rocket can be used to launch Starlink satellites.
options.C: It validates the technology needed for future lunar exploration missions.
options.D: It ensures that Elon Musk will become the world's first trillionaire.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第三段所述，NASA 計劃使用 Starship 執行未來的登月任務，而這次 V3 的成功飛行證明了設計的可行性，這對 NASA 的目標至關重要。選項 A 與 NASA 的登月任務無直接關聯；選項 B 雖然 SpaceX 有經營 Starlink，但這不是 NASA 關注的重點；選項 D 是關於馬斯克的個人財富，而非 NASA 的任務需求。

quiz.question: In the fifth paragraph, what does the word 'debut' mean in the context of the company's IPO?

options.A: A first public appearance or entry into a market.
options.B: A final performance before closing a business.
options.C: A formal apology for previous failures.
options.D: A secret meeting between investors and the CEO.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。在第五段提到「largest stock market debut」，指的是 SpaceX 首次公開募股（IPO），即公司股票第一次在公開市場上架交易，意指「首次亮相」或「初次登場」。選項 B 與 IPO 的定義相反；選項 C 與 D 在文中完全沒有根據，且與「debut」的商業含義不符。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of the article regarding the Starship V3 mission?

options.A: The mission was a total failure due to engine issues and the rocket's explosion.
options.B: The mission highlights the risks of investing in private space companies.
options.C: The mission represents a major technological advancement and a positive step for future space travel.
options.D: The mission was primarily designed to make Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章整體基調在強調 Starship V3 的成功，儘管有小問題，但相比前幾代版本已有顯著進步，且為未來的太空探索（如登月）奠定了基礎。選項 A 忽略了文中提到任務「largely successful」的事實；選項 B 文中並未討論投資風險；選項 D 雖然提到馬斯克的財富，但那是作為背景資訊，並非文章的核心主旨。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
